cases.name: lexical-layout.simple-layout
cases.notes: Representative layout-sensitive let binding in the executable subset.

cases.name: lexical-layout.nested-layout
cases.notes: Nested layout blocks in conditional and let expressions.

cases.name: lexical-layout.explicit-braces
cases.notes: Explicit brace and semicolon layout form.

cases.name: expressions.arithmetic
cases.notes: Arithmetic precedence over executable Int.

cases.name: expressions.subtraction
cases.notes: Subtraction through the executable Num Int dictionary.

cases.name: expressions.multiplication
cases.notes: Multiplication through the executable Num Int dictionary.

cases.name: expressions.division
cases.notes: Checked concrete Int division primitive.

cases.name: expressions.comparison
cases.notes: Ord comparisons and lazy Boolean conjunction.

cases.name: expressions.if-expression
cases.notes: If-expression desugaring to Bool case.

cases.name: expressions.lambda-application
cases.notes: Lambda expression applied immediately.

cases.name: expressions.let-expression
cases.notes: Local let binding in expression position.

cases.name: expressions.case-bool
cases.notes: Explicit case over Bool.

cases.name: expressions.where-groups
cases.notes: Function and case-alternative where declaration groups lower through Core/STG/native execution for the currently accepted where syntax.

cases.name: expressions.where-layout
cases.notes: Report-shaped line-broken where groups on function bindings and case alternatives lower through Core/STG/native execution.

cases.name: expressions.sections
cases.notes: Left and right operator sections desugar to typed Core lambdas, including lazy Boolean section semantics.

cases.name: expressions.user-defined-operators
cases.notes: User-defined symbolic and backtick operators compile as ordinary functions with local fixity and Prelude-shadowing behavior.

cases.name: modules.single-module-main
cases.notes: Single explicit Main module.

cases.name: modules.two-module-import
cases.notes: Same-directory module graph with qualified import, export list, and constructor child import.

cases.name: modules.implicit-prelude
cases.notes: Modules without an explicit Prelude import receive names as if by import Prelude.

cases.name: modules.explicit-prelude-import
cases.notes: An explicit Prelude import suppresses the implicit import and restricts unqualified names to the import list.

cases.name: modules.qualified-prelude
cases.notes: A qualified explicit Prelude import suppresses implicit unqualified Prelude names while keeping qualified access.

cases.name: modules.instance-import-export
cases.notes: Instances cross empty export lists and empty import lists through a transitive import chain.

cases.name: modules.standard-library-modules
cases.notes: Implemented generated standard-library modules import through ModuleInterface with explicit export lists, child exports, fixities, and ordinary Prelude-backed semantics.

cases.name: modules.standard-library-scalar-types
cases.notes: Generated Data.Int, Data.Word, and Foreign.C.Types interfaces expose the currently supported scalar type-name surface.

cases.name: declarations.top-level-function
cases.notes: Top-level function binding.

cases.name: declarations.top-level-signature
cases.notes: Top-level type signature checked against binding.

cases.name: declarations.fixity-expression-signature
cases.notes: A local fixity declaration affects infix reassociation and expression type signatures check before native execution.

cases.name: declarations.multiple-definitions
cases.notes: Multiple top-level value definitions in one module.

cases.name: declarations.pattern-binding
cases.notes: Top-level non-variable pattern bindings emit selector bindings and compile natively.

cases.name: declarations.newtype-wrapper
cases.notes: Newtype constructor and pattern typing through the current boxed executable representation.

cases.name: laziness.const-unused-div
cases.notes: Unused function argument is not forced.

cases.name: laziness.lazy-let-unused-div
cases.notes: Unused let binding is not forced.

cases.name: laziness.forced-division-by-zero
cases.notes: Forced division by zero must compile but exit nonzero at runtime.

cases.name: adts.known-constructor-case
cases.notes: User ADT constructor and case alternative.

cases.name: adts.maybe-constructor-case
cases.notes: Maybe-like polymorphic constructor case.

cases.name: adts.record-fields
cases.notes: Record declaration labels, selector functions, complete record construction, and record pattern matching.

cases.name: adts.record-update
cases.notes: Record update syntax lowers through typed Core/STG/native execution, updates multiple fields, composes with record construction, and preserves laziness of untouched fields.

cases.name: adts.record-update-missing-constructor-field
cases.notes: A record update whose labels are valid for the datatype but not present on the runtime constructor compiles and fails by missing case alternative at runtime.

cases.name: lists-tuples.list-length
cases.notes: List literal and generated Prelude length.

cases.name: lists-tuples.tuple-case
cases.notes: Tuple expression and tuple pattern case.

cases.name: recursion.factorial
cases.notes: Recursive top-level function over Int.

cases.name: patterns.guards-as-patterns
cases.notes: As-pattern alias and guarded equations.

cases.name: types.polymorphic-id
cases.notes: Explicitly typed polymorphic identity instantiated at Int.

cases.name: types.numeric-defaulting
cases.notes: Ambiguous numeric and Eq constraints default to executable Int.

cases.name: types.monomorphism-restriction
cases.notes: Unsigned nullary value binding follows the documented executable-subset monomorphism/defaulting policy.

cases.name: types.type-synonym-expansion
cases.notes: Type synonyms expand through signatures and constructor fields before Core/native lowering.

cases.name: typeclasses.user-dictionary
cases.notes: User single-parameter class, concrete instance, and dictionary-passed method call.

cases.name: typeclasses.constraint-synonym
cases.notes: Explicit class constraint whose argument is normalized through a type synonym before dictionary elaboration.

cases.name: prelude.prelude-lists
cases.notes: Generated Prelude list functions over the supported executable subset.

cases.name: prelude.append
cases.notes: Prelude (++) appends lists and strings and works through infix use, prefix use, and operator sections.

cases.name: prelude.foldl
cases.notes: Generated Prelude foldl covers left-to-right accumulator order, polymorphic accumulator/list element types, empty-list behavior, and lazy ignored accumulators.

cases.name: prelude.functions
cases.notes: Generated Prelude function-completion slice covers ($), (.), flip, head, tail, null, fst, and snd through Core, STG, native, and Char-list String output.

cases.name: prelude.head-empty
cases.notes: Generated Prelude head preserves Haskell's partial selector behavior by compiling an empty-list case to a native no-match runtime failure.

cases.name: prelude.prelude-classes
cases.notes: Built-in Eq, Ord, Num, and Bool Prelude methods.

cases.name: prelude.char-runtime
cases.notes: Boxed Char literals, Char literal case alternatives, and the built-in Eq Char dictionary lower through native STG.

cases.name: prelude.char-main
cases.notes: Executable scalar Char main unboxes through the native runtime and prints as a character.

cases.name: prelude.string-char-list
cases.notes: Source string literals and string literal patterns lower to ordinary [Char] lists; length and show output consume them as lists.

cases.name: prelude.string-output
cases.notes: Direct string literals and list functions over strings feed native putStrLn/print through ordinary [Char] values.

cases.name: prelude.string-show-output
cases.notes: Built-in Show Int and Show Bool results are native [Char] values consumable by putStrLn.

cases.name: prelude.string-char-patterns
cases.notes: Native execution covers explicit Char cons patterns and string literal patterns over source string values.

cases.name: prelude.broad-show
cases.notes: Broader Show coverage for Char, String, generic lists, nested string lists, print, and defaulted numeric list elements.

cases.name: prelude.arithmetic-sequences
cases.notes: Executable arithmetic sequences for Int and Char, including ascending, descending, and lazily consumed open Int ranges.

cases.name: prelude.numeric-hierarchy
cases.notes: Executable Real Int and Integral Int dictionaries cover quot/rem/div/mod, pair-returning quotient methods, toInteger, integer-rational conversion, and Integer default declarations.

cases.name: prelude.enum-bounded
cases.notes: Public Enum Int/Char and Bounded Int/Char/Bool dictionaries, including defaulting through Num+Enum, compile and run natively.

cases.name: prelude.list-comprehensions
cases.notes: Executable list comprehensions over Int and Char ranges, guards, nested generators, let qualifiers, tuple patterns, and refutable Maybe patterns.

cases.name: io.printing
cases.notes: Current supported IO printing slice with exact stdout.

cases.name: io.normal-examples
cases.notes: Normal IO examples after real String and Show support: do-bind, explicit >>=, putStrLn, and print over Char, String, and lists.

cases.name: io.getline
cases.notes: Native getLine reads stdin line-by-line, strips line terminators, returns ordinary String values, and composes with append, length, putStrLn, print, and do-bind.

cases.name: io.fail
cases.notes: Explicit IO fail compiles through the Monad IO dictionary and exits nonzero at runtime.

cases.name: egglog.known-constructor
cases.notes: Known-constructor Core optimizer case must agree with --no-egglog.

cases.name: typeclasses.monad
cases.notes: Haskell 2010 Monad dictionaries drive IO, Maybe, and list do-notation, including fail on refutable do-bind patterns.

cases.name: typeclasses.monad-explicit-fail
cases.notes: Explicit fail uses the Monad Maybe and Monad list dictionaries without going through refutable do-bind desugaring.

cases.name: ffi.static-ccall
cases.notes: Static foreign import ccall declarations lower through LLVM, link against C helpers, marshal Int/Bool/Char values, and sequence IO effects.

cases.name: ffi.pointer-address
cases.notes: Ptr/FunPtr imports cover static data addresses, function addresses, pointer arguments, pointer results, mutation through IO, and function-pointer arguments.

cases.name: ffi.dynamic-wrapper
cases.notes: dynamic imports emit indirect FunPtr calls and wrapper imports create C-callable callbacks that re-enter Haskell closures.

cases.name: ffi.foreign-export
cases.notes: foreign export ccall generates C-callable entrypoints for supported pure and IO functions and links C helpers that call back into Haskell.

cases.name: ffi.stable-foreignptr-finalizers
cases.notes: StablePtr and manual ForeignPtr APIs compile natively with stable pointer dereference/free, casts, withForeignPtr, finalizer registration, reverse-order dispatch, and idempotent finalization.

cases.name: negative.type-error
cases.notes: Ill-typed arithmetic must fail with a type diagnostic.

cases.name: negative.unbound-variable
cases.notes: Unbound variable must fail during renaming.

cases.name: negative.duplicate-binding
cases.notes: Duplicate top-level binding must be rejected.

cases.name: negative.bad-module-import
cases.notes: Missing same-directory imported module must fail module graph loading.

cases.name: negative.malformed-layout
cases.notes: Malformed layout must fail during Haskell 2010 parsing.

cases.name: negative.malformed-where-layout
cases.notes: Malformed where-group layout must fail during Haskell 2010 parsing.

cases.name: negative.misindented-where-keyword
cases.notes: A line-broken where keyword at the enclosing declaration column is rejected instead of being attached to the preceding RHS.

cases.name: negative.constructor-operator-binding
cases.notes: Colon-prefixed constructor operators are rejected in value-operator binding syntax.

cases.name: negative.invalid-pattern-binding
cases.notes: A top-level non-variable pattern binding with duplicate binders is rejected by pattern-binder validation.

cases.name: negative.duplicate-function-parameter
cases.notes: Duplicate source binders in one function equation are rejected before typechecking.

cases.name: negative.impossible-case-pattern
cases.notes: A case alternative whose pattern is impossible for the scrutinee type is rejected with a type diagnostic.

cases.name: negative.duplicate-monad-io
cases.notes: User instances must not overlap or duplicate the Haskell 2010 built-in Monad IO dictionary.

cases.name: negative.record-update-ambiguous-fields
cases.notes: Record update labels must all belong to the same datatype.

cases.name: negative.record-update-duplicate-field
cases.notes: Duplicate field labels in a record update are rejected.

cases.name: negative.record-update-invalid-field
cases.notes: A field selector from another datatype is rejected for the scrutinee record type.

cases.name: negative.record-update-non-record
cases.notes: A concrete non-record scrutinee cannot be updated with record fields.

cases.name: negative.missing-instance-import
cases.notes: A class and type import do not make an instance visible unless an import chain reaches the instance-declaring module.

cases.name: negative.prelude-empty-import
cases.notes: An explicit empty Prelude import suppresses the implicit Prelude import.

cases.name: negative.prelude-qualified-unqualified-use
cases.notes: A qualified Prelude import does not bind Prelude names for unqualified use.

cases.name: negative.ffi-foreignptr-direct
cases.notes: ForeignPtr is an ownership wrapper, not a direct C ABI argument type; code must use withForeignPtr to expose Ptr.

cases.name: negative.ffi-wrapper-bad-shape
cases.notes: foreign import ccall wrapper must wrap a function type and return IO (FunPtr ft).

cases.name: negative.ffi-export-missing-target
cases.notes: foreign export must reference an existing top-level Haskell binding.

cases.name: negative.ffi-unsupported-callconv
cases.notes: Reserved non-C calling conventions are represented structurally but rejected until target-specific support is implemented.

cases.name: negative.derived-enum-field-constructor
cases.notes: Derived Enum is rejected for data declarations whose constructors carry fields.

cases.name: negative.derived-bounded-mixed-constructors
cases.expectedDiagnosticCategory: requires an enumeration or a single constructor
cases.notes: Derived Bounded is rejected for declarations that are neither all-nullary enumerations nor single-constructor products.

cases.name: negative.invalid-default-type
cases.notes: Default declarations reject non-numeric default types instead of silently falling back.

cases.name: typeclasses.superclass-default-method
cases.notes: Superclass dictionaries, default class methods, and superclass projection from local dictionaries compile and run.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-eq
cases.notes: Derived Eq synthesizes structural equality and inequality for nullary/product constructors, parameterized data, recursive data, newtype, String fields, and list-backed instance contexts.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-ord
cases.notes: Derived Ord synthesizes Haskell 2010 declaration-order constructor comparison, lexicographic product-field comparison, superclass Eq dictionaries, recursive instances, newtype ordering, String fields, and structural Ord lists.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-show
cases.expectedStdout:
Off
Box ('x')
Name ("aa")
Node (Leaf ('a')) (Leaf ('b'))
Years (7)
Person { age = (42), label = ("Ada") }
[Box (True),Box (False)]
cases.notes: Derived Show synthesizes executable-subset Show dictionaries for nullary constructors, products, recursive data, newtypes, records, String fields, and list-backed contexts.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-enum
cases.notes: Derived Enum synthesizes dictionaries for nullary-constructor data declarations, including constructor indices, succ/pred, toEnum/fromEnum, range methods, and range syntax over derived enumeration types.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-enum-runtime-error
cases.notes: Derived Enum succ on the maximum constructor compiles but fails at runtime, matching the Haskell 2010 derived Enum bounds rule.

cases.name: typeclasses.derived-bounded
cases.expectedStdout:
0
3
Pair (False) (North)
Pair (True) (West)
Record { low = (False), high = (North) }
Record { low = (True), high = (West) }
Flag (False)
Flag (True)
cases.notes: Derived Bounded synthesizes enumeration bounds and single-constructor field-wise minBound/maxBound dictionaries for data, record, newtype, and parameterized product declarations.

cases.name: unsupported.instance-context
cases.notes: Instance contexts are intentionally outside the current concrete-instance dictionary slice.

cases.name: unsupported.method-specific-constraint
cases.notes: Method-specific class constraints are rejected by the explicit class-constraint placeholder.

cases.name: unsupported.read-class
cases.notes: TC-016 documents Read as a deliberate Prelude/typeclass deviation until ReadS, readsPrec/readList, lexical parsing, and derived Read are implemented coherently.

cases.name: unsupported.constrained-expression-signature
cases.notes: Constrained expression signatures are parsed but deliberately rejected until the broader local constraint surface is implemented.

cases.name: unsupported.kind-checking
cases.notes: Partial type-constructor use must be rejected by kind checking.

cases.name: unsupported.handle-io
cases.notes: Handle-based IO remains beyond the current line-oriented stdin/stdout IO slice.

cases.name: patterns.irrefutable-pattern
cases.notes: Irrefutable/lazy patterns match without forcing the scrutinee and force mismatches only through demanded binders.

cases.name: patterns.unit-wildcard
cases.notes: Unit and wildcard function patterns compile natively without forcing an unused argument.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-array
cases.notes: Data.Array remains reserved until array construction, indexing, updates, and Ix integration are implemented under LIB-005.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-bits
cases.notes: Data.Bits remains reserved until bit classes and operations are implemented under LIB-006.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-char
cases.notes: Data.Char remains reserved until character classification, conversion, numeric, and string representation functions are implemented under LIB-004.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-complex
cases.notes: Data.Complex remains reserved until the floating numeric tower and Complex API are implemented under LIB-008.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-ix
cases.notes: Data.Ix remains reserved until Ix class methods and derived Ix support are implemented under LIB-005.

cases.name: unsupported.library-data-ratio
cases.notes: Data.Ratio remains reserved until exact Ratio/Rational representation and operations are implemented under LIB-007.

cases.name: unsupported.library-foreign-c-error
cases.notes: Foreign.C.Error remains reserved until errno and IO-error marshalling are implemented under FFI-013.

cases.name: unsupported.library-foreign-marshal
cases.notes: Foreign.Marshal.* remains reserved until allocation, array, error, and utility marshalling APIs are implemented under FFI-013.

cases.name: unsupported.library-foreign-storable
cases.notes: Foreign.Storable remains reserved until Storable dictionaries and memory access primitives are implemented under FFI-013.

cases.name: unsupported.library-numeric
cases.notes: Numeric remains reserved until Report-compatible numeric showing, reading, and miscellaneous helpers are implemented under LIB-010.

cases.name: unsupported.library-system-environment
cases.notes: System.Environment remains reserved until process environment APIs are implemented under LIB-011.

cases.name: unsupported.library-system-exit
cases.notes: System.Exit remains reserved until ExitCode and exit actions are implemented under LIB-011.

cases.name: unsupported.library-system-io-error
cases.notes: System.IO.Error remains reserved until rich IO error types, predicates, and catch/throw behavior are implemented under IO-011.

cases.name: unsupported.package-module-search-path
cases.notes: Package and unimplemented library module search paths are not implemented; imports only load generated standard-library interfaces or same-directory files.
